summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened his message by expressing a desire to live in the overflow of God's goodness, mercy, favor, and blessing, not just experiencing it but having it spill out onto others. He emphasized that God, revealed as El Shaddai (the God of more than enough), desires this overflow for His people when they serve Him faithfully. The sermon was anchored in Luke 5:17-26, the story of friends who brought a paralyzed man to Jesus. From this passage, Pastor Travis outlined three prayers for living in the overflow. First, he prayed for God's explosive, dunamis power to fill and transform lives, marriages, and the church, noting that true spiritual power attracts even critics. Second, he challenged the congregation to overflow with generosity, teaching that the harvest we reap is determined by the seeds we sow and that we are blessed to be a blessing. Third, he called for a heart that overflows with compassion for the hurting, specifically urging men to rise up as spiritual leaders who actively bring broken people to Jesus. The message concluded with a direct application to the church's upcoming Resurrection Sunday outreach at a stadium, calling for investment in eternal things and personal invitation of friends and family to encounter Christ.
mainPoints: Pray to overflow with God's dunamis power, which transforms lives and attracts others.
Pray to overflow with generosity, understanding that we reap a harvest from the seeds we sow.
Pray to overflow with a heart for the hurting, actively bringing people to Jesus, with a specific call for men to lead spiritually.

versesMentioned.text: Surely your goodness and love will follow me all the days of my life, and I will dwell in the house of the LORD forever.
The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
And my God will meet all your needs according to the riches of his glory in Christ Jesus.

One day Jesus was teaching, and Pharisees and teachers of the law were sitting there. They had come from every village of Galilee and from Judea and Jerusalem. And the power of the Lord was with Jesus to heal the sick. Some men came carrying a paralyzed man on a mat and tried to take him into the house to lay him before Jesus. When they could not find a way to do this because of the crowd, they went up on the roof and lowered him on his mat through the tiles into the middle of the crowd, right in front of Jesus. When Jesus saw their faith, he said, 'Friend, your sins are forgiven.' ... He said to the paralyzed man, 'I tell you, get up, take your mat and go home.' Immediately he stood up in front of them, took what he had been lying on and went home praising God.
So you must be careful to do everything they tell you. But do not do what they do, for they do not practice what they preach.
But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes on you; and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, and in all Judea and Samaria, and to the ends of the earth.
Remember this: Whoever sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and whoever sows generously will also reap generously.
Give, and it will be given to you. A good measure, pressed down, shaken together and running over, will be poured into your lap. For with the measure you use, it will be measured to you.

Command those who are rich in this present world not to be arrogant nor to put their hope in wealth, which is so uncertain, but to put their hope in God, who richly provides us with everything for our enjoyment. Command them to do good, to be rich in good deeds, and to be generous and willing to share. In this way they will lay up treasure for themselves as a firm foundation for the coming age, so that they may take hold of the life that is truly life.

Monday.scripture: Philippians 4:19 - 'And my God will meet all your needs according to the riches of his glory in Christ Jesus.'
Monday.focus: God as El Shaddai, your more-than-enough provider.
Monday.action: Identify one area of need or lack in your life and consciously choose to trust God's promise to provide.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for being my El Shaddai. I trust you to meet my needs today and overflow your provision into my life.

Tuesday.scripture: Acts 1:8 - 'But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes on you; and you will be my witnesses...'
Tuesday.focus: Receiving and walking in God's dunamis (explosive) power.
Tuesday.action: Pray for God's power to fill you, then step out in one act of faith or courage you've been hesitant about.
Tuesday.prayer: Holy Spirit, fill me with your power today. Let your dunamis work through me to be a witness and bring change.

Wednesday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 9:6 - 'Whoever sows generously will also reap generously.'
Wednesday.focus: The principle of generous sowing for an overflowing harvest.
Wednesday.action: Perform one act of intentional generosity today—it could be with your time, resources, or encouragement.
Wednesday.prayer: God, give me a generous heart. Help me to sow good seeds today, trusting you for the harvest you promise.

Thursday.scripture: Luke 6:38 - 'Give, and it will be given to you. A good measure, pressed down, shaken together and running over...'
Thursday.focus: Living with an open hand, ready to give and receive blessing.
Thursday.action: Look for an opportunity to help someone in a practical way without expecting anything in return.
Thursday.prayer: Father, open my eyes to see needs around me. Let my life be a channel of your blessing, giving freely as you have given to me.

Friday.scripture: Luke 5:20 - 'When Jesus saw their faith, he said, "Friend, your sins are forgiven."'
Friday.focus: Having faith that brings others to Jesus.
Friday.action: Think of one person in your life who needs Jesus. Commit to praying for them and look for a way to encourage them this week.
Friday.prayer: Jesus, give me a heart for the lost and hurting. Use my faith to help bring someone closer to you. Show me how to be a 'friend' who carries others to your feet.

Saturday.scripture: 1 Timothy 6:18-19 - 'Command them to do good, to be rich in good deeds, and to be generous and willing to share. In this way they will lay up treasure for themselves as a firm foundation for the coming age...'
Saturday.action: Make a decision to invest in God's kingdom—this could be through financial giving, committing to serve, or dedicating time to prayer.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, help me to see my resources through your eyes. I choose to invest in what lasts forever. Store up treasure in heaven through my life today.
